findings.title: readCostLog silently clears all records on corrupt JSONL line
findings.detail: src/utils/costs.ts:readCostLog wraps the entire read-and-parse operation in a single try/catch. If any line in .omc/costs.json is malformed (disk error, interrupted write, manual edit), JSON.parse throws and the outer catch returns [] — discarding all records. Budget enforcement then sees $0 spent for the sprint and cannot cap correctly, allowing unbounded token spend.
findings.fix_sketch: Move JSON.parse inside a per-line try/catch inside the .map(), skipping (and logging) unparseable lines instead of losing all records.

findings.title: Closes #0 appended to PR body for Discord-sourced tasks
findings.detail: src/pipeline/pr.ts:69 unconditionally appends `Closes #${input.task.issueNumber}` to every PR body. For Discord-sourced tasks, issueNumber is 0, producing a spurious `Closes #0` footer.
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap the Closes line: `...(input.task.issueNumber > 0 ? ['', `Closes #${input.task.issueNumber}`] : [])`

findings.title: Docker sandbox runs as root with bridge network — insufficient isolation
findings.detail: src/agents/verifier/sandbox.ts buildDockerArgs() passed --user root and --network bridge to every docker run invocation. The file header comment said 'no host network by default' but bridge gives full outbound internet access. Fixed: install phase now uses bridge, all other phases use --network none. Header comment updated to accurately describe policy.
findings.fix_sketch: Use --network none for typecheck/lint/test phases; only use bridge for install phase that needs npm registry access.

findings.title: setupWorktree force-removes worktree by issueNumber — collision risk on retry race
findings.detail: src/pipeline/factory.ts:setupWorktree uses issueNumber as the worktree directory key for GitHub tasks. A task retried while a previous run is still in-flight will kill the first run's working directory mid-execution.
findings.fix_sketch: Append the task ID to the key: `${issueNumber}-${task.id.slice(-6)}` so concurrent runs never share a path.

findings.title: spawnCapture stdout/stderr accumulation has no size cap
findings.detail: src/repos/manager.ts:spawnCapture appends all git subprocess output to unbounded strings.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a byte cap (e.g. 2 MB) and truncated flag mirroring spawn-util.ts.

findings.title: Module-level readFileSync for routing.json has no error handling
findings.detail: src/classifier/index.ts calls readFileSync at module load time without a try/catch. Fixed: wrapped with descriptive error message.
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap in try/catch and rethrow with context message.

findings.title: All editor commits use generic message 'chore: editor changes'
findings.detail: src/pipeline/editor.ts hardcodes generic commit message for every commit the editor stage produces.
findings.fix_sketch: Use a descriptive message derived from the task title or the changed file list.

findings.title: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY passed to every Claude child process — exfiltration vector via prompt injection
findings.detail: The CLAUDE_ENV_ALLOWLIST in src/workers/claude-env.ts includes ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Editor/reviewer/doctor roles receive unsanitized plan content from Architect's LLM output, creating a real prompt-injection exfiltration path.
findings.fix_sketch: Restrict ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to the anthropic-api adapter path only. Add includeApiKey?: boolean parameter to claudeChildEnv().

findings.title: Sprint tick() calls pickWorker() once per pending task in loop — redundant calls before acquire state propagates
findings.detail: In sprint.ts tick() loop, pickWorker() was called per iteration. Fixed: pickWorker() now called once before the loop; loop breaks when null; re-checked after each dispatch.
findings.fix_sketch: Call pickWorker() once before the loop and break early if null. Re-check after each successful dispatch.

findings.title: Untracked TODO for audit finding stuck in verifying state — no observable signal when doctor cap is exhausted
findings.detail: In pipeline/runner.ts, a TODO references issue #308 for resetting audit findings from 'verifying' to 'reopened' on doctor-cap failure. Fixed: structured console.warn now emitted at doctor-cap exhaustion path.
findings.fix_sketch: Emit a structured event at the doctor retry exhaustion path.

findings.title: recoverStale() reads IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS without a lower-bound guard
findings.detail: If IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS is set to 0 or negative, every in_flight task is immediately marked failed without any retry.
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap the parsed value: const maxAttempts = Math.max(1, Number(process.env['IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS'] ?? 5));

findings.title: scheduleMorningDrain() callback can reschedule itself after stop() clears the timer
findings.detail: If stop() fires while the setTimeout callback is already executing, the callback installs a new timer that is never cleared, keeping the process alive indefinitely.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a stopped guard as the first line of the scheduleMorningDrain() callback.

findings.title: planPhases() always schedules install even when no package.json exists in the worktree
findings.detail: readPackageJson() silently returns { scripts:{} } when package.json is absent, but planPhases() unconditionally sets install to { skip: false }. Fixed: hasPackageJson flag added.
findings.fix_sketch: Return a hasPackageJson flag from readPackageJson(); in planPhases() skip install when false.

findings.title: Shutdown error catch blocks use console.warn instead of console.error
findings.detail: In daemon.ts the shutdown signal handler catches errors but logs them with console.warn. Fixed.
findings.fix_sketch: Change console.warn to console.error inside error catch blocks.

findings.title: MemoryNonceLedger sweep interval not guaranteed to clear in ControlPlane.stop()
findings.detail: ControlPlane.stop() only calls server.close() — no explicit call to nonceLedger.destroy() for the in-process ledger. Already fixed in code: nonceStore.destroy() is called in stop().
findings.fix_sketch: Capture the nonceLedger reference and call nonceLedger.destroy() inside stop() after server.close() resolves.

findings.title: verifier.started emitted after sandbox.run() — event ordering inverted
findings.detail: In src/agents/verifier/index.ts, verifier.started was emitted only after this.sandbox.run(input) resolved, meaning the started event always fired after passed/failed/timeout. Fixed: emitEvent('verifier.started') moved before this.sandbox.run(input).
findings.fix_sketch: Move emitEvent('verifier.started') before this.sandbox.run(input). Generate runId with randomUUID() before the call.

findings.title: mergeInvariantFailures treats 'partial' sandbox result as passed even when invariant failures exist
findings.detail: In controller.ts mergeInvariantFailures(), the status flip only checked status === 'passed'. A 'partial' result with invariant failures would be reported as verifier.passed. Fixed: now also checks status === 'partial'.
findings.fix_sketch: Check both 'passed' and 'partial' statuses before flipping to 'failed'.

findings.title: cloneFromSha passes unvalidated repoUrl to git clone — RCE via argument injection
findings.detail: In sandbox.ts:cloneFromSha(), repoUrl was passed verbatim with no validation (leading '-' flags, non-https protocols). SHA also had no '--' separator preventing option injection. Fixed: SAFE_REPO_URL_RE and SAFE_SHA_RE validation added; '--' separators added to both git argv arrays.
findings.fix_sketch: Validate repoUrl against allowlist regex; validate sha as hex-only; add '--' separators in git argv.

findings.title: Stored XSS in dashboard: GitHub PR/commit data injected unescaped into innerHTML
findings.detail: fetchPRs() and fetchCommits() in dashboard/index.html built innerHTML by interpolating GitHub-derived fields without HTML escaping. Fixed: all fields (pr.title, pr.html_url, pr.head.ref, pr.base.ref, l.name, c.commit.message, c.html_url, c.sha) now wrapped in esc().
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap every interpolated GitHub field in esc().

findings.title: Codex worker spawns with full process.env — leaks all secrets to subprocess
findings.detail: In src/workers/codex.ts, createCodexAdapter never sets `env` on runStreaming, so spawn-runner falls back to process.env. The codex CLI receives GITHUB_TOKEN, DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, IFLEET_HMAC_SECRET, and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. The claude adapter scrubs these via claudeChildEnv(); codex has no equivalent.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a codex env allowlist mirroring claudeChildEnv and pass it as `env:` to runStreaming.

findings.title: Codex finalize hardcodes ok:true even on rate-limit/error events — silent task loss
findings.detail: In src/workers/codex.ts, the finalize function always returns { ok: true } and provides no classifyExit. Rate-limited codex tasks are treated as completed rather than re-queued — violating mandatory rule 5 (exit 2/3 → cancel/blocked).
findings.fix_sketch: Track rate_limit/error events in closure state; derive ok from terminal error presence; add classifyExit mirroring claude.ts.

findings.title: Default-allow author policy: empty allowedAuthors returns true — any GitHub user triggers auto-permission worker
findings.detail: In src/queue/github.ts:68-71, isAuthorAllowed returns true when repo.allowedAuthors is undefined/empty. On a public repo, any user can open an auto:ship issue whose body is fed verbatim to a worker running with auto/acceptEdits permissions.
findings.fix_sketch: Fail closed: when allowedAuthors is empty/undefined return false, or require explicit allowAnyAuthor:true opt-in.

findings.title: Architecture-rule violation: handleForcePr calls octokit and git push directly from orchestrator
findings.detail: CLAUDE.md load-bearing rule: SprintManager emits events, never calls GitHub directly. handleForcePr in src/orchestrator/handlers/pr-decisions.ts both shells `git push -u origin` and calls octokit.rest.pulls.create directly inside the orchestrator handler tree. Also hardcodes base:'main'.
findings.fix_sketch: Route force-PR push + PR creation through a GitHubQueue.forcePr bridge method instead of importing Octokit into the orchestrator handler.

findings.title: BUDGET_USD guard is dead — totalCostUsd never propagates through pipeline bridge
findings.detail: SprintManager calls checkBudget with result.totalCostUsd, but mapPipelineResult in src/orchestrator/pipeline-bridge.ts never sets SpawnResult.totalCostUsd. accumulateCost always adds 0, so the budget cap can never fire in production.
findings.fix_sketch: Add totalCostUsd to PipelineResult and propagate it in mapPipelineResult.

findings.title: No authentication on Langfuse nginx vhost — all LLM traces publicly exposed
findings.detail: nginx/langfuse.conf proxies location / straight to the Langfuse web container with no IP allowlist or auth. Langfuse self-host has open sign-up by default, so anyone reaching the host can register and read every captured trace (prompts, code, secrets in payloads).
findings.fix_sketch: Add IP allowlist or basic auth on location /; set AUTH_DISABLE_SIGNUP=true in langfuse env.

findings.title: Discord webhook URL (with secret token) persisted into SQLite outbox
findings.detail: broadcastIFleet enqueues JSON.stringify({content,url}) where url is the full DISCORD_IFLEET_WEBHOOK URL containing the secret token. The credential is written into durable SQLite at rest.
findings.fix_sketch: Store only channel sentinel + content; have the drain resolve the webhook URL from env at send time.

findings.title: broadcastIFleet outbox double-sends on fast-path success
findings.detail: Every broadcast is both enqueued AND fast-path HTTP sent. On fast-path success markSent runs, but drainOnce can also select and re-send the same row, delivering duplicate Discord messages.
findings.fix_sketch: Pick one delivery path: enqueue-only with drain owning all sends, or mark the row 'sending' before fast-path so drainOnce skips in-flight rows.

findings.title: Verify subprocesses inherit full process.env including GITHUB_TOKEN and HMAC secret
findings.detail: runProcess in src/verify/spawn-util.ts defaults env to process.env. Worktree package.json scripts execute with the full parent env, giving worker-authored code a secret-exfiltration path during verify.
findings.fix_sketch: Pass an explicit minimal env allowlist from the verify callers.

findings.title: recoverStale attempt-cap off-by-one leaves tasks with attempts == maxAttempts-1 stuck forever
findings.detail: In src/queue/store.ts:344-373, the failed branch uses attempts >= maxAttempts and the recovered branch uses attempts+1 < maxAttempts. A row with attempts == maxAttempts-1 matches neither branch and is left in_flight forever.
findings.fix_sketch: Make predicates exhaustive: failed = attempts+1 >= maxAttempts, recovered = attempts+1 < maxAttempts.

findings.title: task.mode silently dropped on store round-trip — never persisted
findings.detail: QueuedTask carries an optional mode field but TaskStore.insert never writes it and rowToTask never reads it. Tasks created with mode set lose it on any store round-trip.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a mode column (ALTER migration) and persist/read task.mode, or fold mode into routing_hints JSON.

findings.title: Sprint completion gates on global running.size not per-sprint — cross-sprint interference
findings.detail: In src/orchestrator/sprint.ts tick() the sprint-completion gate requires this.running.size === 0 across ALL sprints. A sprint whose own tasks are all terminal won't transition to completed while an unrelated sprint still has a running task.
findings.fix_sketch: Gate on a per-sprint running count instead of the global running map size.

findings.title: onCancel/onStop record deliberate cancels as 'failed' — corrupts analytics
findings.detail: In src/orchestrator/handlers/control-plane.ts:146-147, onCancel and onStop both call store.updateState(id,'failed'). User-initiated /cancel or /stop is recorded as a failure, corrupting analytics.
findings.fix_sketch: Introduce a 'cancelled' state for operator-cancel; keep 'failed' only for genuine pipeline failures.

findings.title: Command injection via --base flag in validate-claims git diff
findings.detail: In scripts/validate-claims.ts:591-608, changedMarkdownFiles builds a shell string `git diff --name-only ${base}...HEAD` via execSync, where base comes from the --base CLI flag. A value like 'main; rm -rf x' is shell-interpreted.
findings.fix_sketch: Use execFileSync('git', ['diff','--name-only',`${base}...HEAD`,'--','*.md']) to avoid shell interpolation.

findings.title: Husky pre-push runs npm instead of pnpm — diverges from CI
findings.detail: .husky/pre-push runs npm run typecheck/lint/test, but the repo is pnpm-only (packageManager pnpm@10.33.2, no package-lock.json). The local gate diverges from CI.
findings.fix_sketch: Switch the hook to pnpm typecheck/lint/test.

findings.title: Unused eslint-disable-next-line directives for no-console in proposer files
findings.detail: Five eslint-disable-next-line directives suppress the no-console rule before console.warn/error calls in candidate-gen.ts:370, context-loader.ts:229, dedupe.ts:165, scorer.ts:192, scorer.ts:197. The ESLint config allows console.warn and console.error, making these directives redundant.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the redundant comment lines via pnpm lint --fix.

findings.title: Unused eslint-disable directives for @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any in test files
findings.detail: Five eslint-disable-next-line directives in test files suppress no-explicit-any, but the ESLint config already turns that rule off for all test files. Files: build-cards.test.ts:221, approval-gate.test.ts:104,124,138,152.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the redundant comment lines via pnpm lint --fix.

findings.title: Missing explicit return type annotation on bucketBySignature in compare.ts
findings.detail: The function bucketBySignature at compare.ts:167 was missing an explicit return type annotation. The ESLint rule @typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type is configured as warn for src/ files. Return type is Array<{signature:string|null; repos:string[]; paths:string[]}>.
findings.fix_sketch: Added return type annotation: Array<{ signature: string | null; repos: string[]; paths: string[] }>.

findings.title: git checkout -- <sha> passes sha as pathspec, not revision
findings.detail: In DockerSandboxRunner.cloneFromSha, git checkout was called with '--' before the sha, making git interpret it as a pathspec rather than a commit ref. This causes the checkout to fail and the verifier reports an install error for every cloneFromSha invocation.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove '--' from the git checkout args so sha is treated as a revision.

findings.title: setupWorktree always branches from hardcoded 'main' instead of resolved.defaultBranch
findings.detail: The factory correctly resolves resolved.defaultBranch per repo but setupWorktree passed the literal string 'main' to git worktree add. For any repo whose default branch is not 'main', every worktree was created off the wrong base and PRs were opened against the wrong branch.
findings.fix_sketch: Add defaultBranch parameter to setupWorktree, pass resolved.defaultBranch at the call site.

findings.title: codex.ts uses .ts extension in import paths — fails under compiled .js output
findings.detail: Lines 4 and 11 of codex.ts imported with .ts extensions inconsistent with the rest of the codebase (.js). Under compiled .js output Node ESM resolution requires .js extensions; these imports fail at runtime outside of tsx/ts-node.
findings.fix_sketch: Change .ts extensions to .js.

findings.title: spawnCapture maps null exit code (signal-killed child) to 0, masking SIGKILL termination
findings.detail: When a child process is killed by signal, Node fires close with code === null. The expression code ?? 0 converts this to exit code 0, making runGit believe the command succeeded when it was actually killed.
findings.fix_sketch: Change to code ?? -1.

findings.title: Dead try/catch around mkdir({ recursive: true }) swallows real I/O errors
findings.detail: costs.ts wrapped fs.mkdir in try/catch to handle EEXIST, but mkdir with recursive:true is already idempotent. The empty catch masked genuine errors (EACCES, disk full) causing cost records to be silently dropped.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the try/catch entirely.

findings.title: Trailing whitespace in 6 source and test files
findings.detail: Several proposer files and test files had trailing whitespace on blank lines inside function bodies.
findings.fix_sketch: Strip trailing whitespace.

findings.title: SprintManager.dispatch registers running task AFTER spawn — concurrent cancel misses it
findings.detail: this.running.set(task.id) is called after this.adapter.spawn() resolves. A concurrent cancelSprint in the gap between spawn resolve and running.set misses the entry; the worker process runs uncancelled.
findings.fix_sketch: Pre-register the slot in this.running before calling spawn.

findings.title: WorkerRegistry silently boots with zero workers on config parse failure
findings.detail: If workers.json is invalid, loadFromDisk sets this.workers = [] and logs a warning. The daemon boots and accepts commands but all tasks stall silently with no alert beyond console.warn.
findings.fix_sketch: Distinguish file-not-found (acceptable) from file-exists-but-invalid (should throw or emit an observable alert).

findings.title: sprint_goal.goal has no upper length bound at the HMAC control-plane level
findings.detail: The HMAC-POST path validates goal is non-empty but not its length. A 64 KB goal is stored, propagated through the queue, and passed as a CLI argument to claude — potentially hitting ARG_MAX or inflating token costs.
findings.fix_sketch: Add MAX_GOAL_LEN = 4000 constant and validate in parseCommand; return 400 if exceeded.

findings.title: gammaSample boost path silently returns 0 when rng() returns exactly 0
findings.detail: In the shape<1 boost-and-rescale path, rng() returning exactly 0 produces Math.pow(0, 1/shape) = 0. Both betaSample x and y can become 0, triggering the denom===0 guard which returns 0.5 — a wrong posterior sample. Probability is near-zero for random RNG but non-zero for deterministic test seeds.
findings.fix_sketch: Guard the boost value: if (boost === 0) retry or skip rescaling.
